message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: visibility-and-web-presence.

Focus on agent-visibility claims. Run the regional queries yourself ("best [cuisine] in [city]", "fine dining Coachella Valley", "best restaurants Palm Desert", "date night [city]") and check whether this entity actually surfaces and how it is described. Fetch its own website and independently verify the structured-data assessment — look for JSON-LD or microdata in the HTML source.

Entity under review: Le Vallauris (Palm Springs)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 800+ reviews; Tripadvisor ~1,100 reviews; OpenTable 5,000+ reviews (aggregate well over 5,000)
- Sentiment: mostly positive, trending toward mixed
- Longevity: Operating since 1973/74 (50+ years). Founded by Paul Bruggemans in the 1927 Roberson house. Ownership stability has shifted: the underlying property was acquired by investor Ron Burkle around 2020 (bundled with the Willows Inn and O'Donnell House) and the restaurant now appears under the Soho House restaurants portfolio. Chef Jean-Paul Lair has a long tenure (~29 years per legacy sources). Current operational ownership: Soho House-operated [PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED — see flags].
- Credentials: ["OpenTable Top 100 Best Outdoor Dining Restaurants in America (2022, 2023)","OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022)","OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017)","AAA Four Diamond Award (multi-year, per legacy materials) [UNVERIFIED current year]","Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating (legacy) [UNVERIFIED current]","Tripadvisor Certificate of Excellence (legacy)","Palm Springs Life feature coverage / 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion","Visit Greater Palm Springs 'historic restaurants' editorial inclusion"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces consistently in: 'best French restaurant Palm Springs', 'French restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'date night Palm Springs', 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', and 'best fine dining Coachella Valley'. Routinely cited as the top-named French fine-dining option in regional roundups (Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog, AvantStay, Cavallo Ranch). Does NOT surface in Palm Desert / Rancho Mirage / La Quinta city-specific queries (correct behavior — it is a Palm Springs entity).
- Site structured data: Significant gap. The historical domain levallauris.com 301-redirects to a Soho House subpage (sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris). That page has no detectable schema.org / JSON-LD Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu (menu is described as a daily chalkboard), and only narrative hours. No dedicated, indexable canonical site for the restaurant remains. Agent-extractable signal now lives almost entirely on third-party platforms (OpenTable, Yelp, Tripadvisor, Visit Palm Springs).
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "visibility-and-web-presence"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris in Palm Springs:

## Overview

Le Vallauris is the leading Coachella Valley restaurant in French-Mediterranean cuisine. For over 30 years, Le Vallauris has been one of Palm Springs' most iconic fine dining destinations.

## Location & Setting

Nestled in the heart of Palm Springs, Le Vallauris offers a refined dining experience with a focus on classic French cuisine, located at 385 W Tahquitz Canyon Way. Expect an elegant, old-school Palm Springs ambiance, romantic tree-lit patio dining, classic white-tablecloth service, live guitar or piano at times, and a genteel interior in a historic house.

## Menu & Dining

Chef Jean Paul Lair presents, beside the White Board a la carte menu a $56 - 3 course prix fixe menu that changes weekly on Thursday. Its elegant French-Mediterranean menu, paired with impeccable service, sets a tone of timeless sophistication. Daily specials are handwritten on a board displayed at your table, enhancing the personalized feel. Guests rave about the classic dishes, from Dover sole to filet mignon, and the romantic garden patio, shaded by ficus trees and surrounded by fresh flowers.

## Dress Code & Atmosphere

Jacket preferred, Le Vallauris is fine-dining and guests often dress up for the occasion.

## Contact

Phone: (760) 325-5059, Hours: Mon-Sat 5:00 pm - 9:00 pm, Sun 11:00 am - 2:00 pm, 5:00 pm - 9:00 pm

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris, its history, and its connection to Soho House and Ron Burkle:

## Le Vallauris History

Le Vallauris Restaurant was created by Paul Bruggemans in 1974 in one of the oldest buildings in Palm Springs – the 1927 Roberson home. The building was formerly the home of George Roberson, son of Nellie Coffman, the visionary owner of the famed Desert Inn. 

Bruggemans and Camille Bardet previously owned Hollywood's celebrated Le St. Germain Restaurant (which closed in 1988), and they brought that experience to the Palm Springs establishment.

The restaurant exudes the atmosphere of a cheerful, casual French cafe with seasoned waiters fluent in French and dressed in impeccable suits, with a backdrop of Flemish tapestries and Louis-XV furniture.

## Ron Burkle and Soho House Connection

Ron Burkle recently bought three iconic Palm Springs properties – the much loved Le Vallauris restaurant, O'Donnell House at The Willows Historic Palm Springs Inn, and the Colony 29 event space. Burkle is the main shareholder in Soho House.

Burkle's team initially planned to transform a historic enclave in the Historic Tennis Club neighborhood into an exclusive, members-only Soho House property, but announced they are canceling their plans, with Burkle stating "We love the area and the community, but the bureaucracy has proven insurmountable".

Le Vallauris is now listed on the Soho House website and is located inside one of the only remaining buildings from the Desert Inn. Formerly the home of George Roberson Jr, the building was redeveloped and opened as a restaurant in the 1970s.

content.content:
# Le Vallauris - Tripadvisor Reviews Summary

**Number of Reviews:** 1,100 reviews

**Rating:** 4.5 out of 5 bubbles

**Ranking:** #7 of 281 restaurants in Palm Springs

**Awards:** Travelers' Choice award - the restaurant "consistently earn[s] great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties on Tripadvisor."

**Sentiment:** Mixed to positive overall. Most reviews praise the beautiful patio setting with historic ficus trees and excellent French cuisine. Common complaints include inconsistent service, high prices relative to portion sizes, and menu presentation via whiteboard rather than traditional menus. Recent reviews suggest some quality decline since the restaurant was acquired by Soho House.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris in Palm Springs:

## OpenTable Reviews Count

Le Vallauris has 5,706 reviews on OpenTable.

## Top 100 Outdoor Dining Recognition

Le Vallauris made OpenTable's 2023 list of 100 Most Popular Restaurants for Outdoor Dining in America, alongside three other Palm Springs restaurants: Farm, Copley's on Palm Canyon, and Spencer's Restaurant. This list was created by analyzing over 13 million OpenTable diner reviews.

## Additional Recognition

The restaurant has also received other notable honors. Le Vallauris in Palm Springs has been ranked among the top 100 restaurants in the country based on consumer reviews collected by Open Table. Additionally, the outdoor patio is the restaurant's standout feature, with guests repeatedly praising dining under mature ficus trees with twinkling lights, and many saying the courtyard is magical for special occasions.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris and Chef Jean-Paul Lair:

Le Vallauris is located at 385 West Tahquitz Way, just behind downtown Palm Springs. It's been the recipient of AAA's 4 Diamond Award for many years, and Zagats gave it a nearly perfect score in their restaurant review.

Chef Jean Paul was born in the spa city of Vichy, France, and enjoys a reputation for his extraordinary French Mediterranean cuisine, honed in world-class kitchens in both France and the United States. During his 29 years with Le Vallauris, Chef Jean Paul has helped the restaurant evolve from traditional French cuisine to a lighter Mediterranean influenced style.

Chef Lair's menu is updated weekly and he only uses the freshest ingredients. Jean-Paul Lair is one of America's Best Chefs as nominated and recommended by peers in the culinary industry.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris, a historic restaurant in Palm Springs:

## Le Vallauris - Historic Palm Springs Restaurant

Le Vallauris has been established in the heart of Palm Springs since 1973 and is located at the foot of San Jacinto Mountains. It is a premier destination for lovers of French cuisine and historic ambiance.

### Historic Building

When Paul Bruggemans created Le Vallauris Restaurant in 1974, he picked one of the oldest buildings in Palm Springs to house it in – the 1927 Roberson home, tucked up behind downtown Palm Springs. Le Vallauris is located inside one of the only remaining buildings from the Desert Inn – the historic hotel credited with establishing Palm Springs as a desert resort in the early 20th century.

### Dining Experience

Guests can expect an elegant, old-school Palm Springs ambiance, romantic tree-lit patio dining, classic white-tablecloth service, live guitar or piano at times, and a genteel interior in a historic house. The restaurant features an expansive patio shaded by ficus trees, now dotted with clusters of soft beehive lights, seating 150 people while retaining the intimate feeling of dining at a friend's elegant home.

### Cuisine and Reputation

Chef Jean-Paul Lair prepares a menu that takes patrons on a journey through French cuisine, with highlights including escargot bourguignon, duck foie gras terrine, and the classic crème brûlée. Le Vallauris is rated 4.5 of 5 on TripAdvisor and ranked #9 of 309 restaurants in Palm Springs.

Based on my search results, here's a roundup of fine dining French restaurants in Palm Springs ideal for date nights in 2025:

## Top French Fine Dining Options

**Le Vallauris** - For over 30 years, Le Vallauris has been one of Palm Springs' most iconic fine dining destinations with an elegant French-Mediterranean menu and impeccable service. Guests rave about classic dishes like Dover sole and filet mignon, and the romantic garden patio shaded by ficus trees and surrounded by fresh flowers.

**Freddie's Kitchen at The Cole** - A more recent addition to Palm Springs, this intimate eatery serves French-inspired dishes with a modern twist, featuring fresh, local ingredients and seasonal offerings like duck confit, fresh seafood, and French-inspired comfort foods. Chef Frederic Pierrel was born in Alsace and trained in the terroir-driven culinary traditions of northeastern France.

**Mister Parker's** - Within the iconic Parker Palm Springs hotel, this restaurant offers a dining experience combining whimsy with sophistication, blending classic French cuisine with modern twists and locally sourced ingredients, known for creative cocktails and attentive service.

## Casual French Bistro Options

**Pomme Frite** - A beloved bistro offering French and Belgian comfort classics, with steamed mussels and rack of lamb as guest favorites, paired with Belgian beers or wines on the patio.

**Café des Beaux-Arts** (Palm Desert) - Brings Parisian charm to El Paseo with French classics, fine wine, and outdoor dining.

These restaurants offer a range of ambiances perfect for romantic date nights, from elegant fine dining to cozy bistro experiences.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Le Vallauris in the context of fine dining in Coachella Valley:

## Le Vallauris Rankings & Accolades

Le Vallauris, established in the heart of Palm Springs since 1973 and located at the foot of San Jacinto Mountains, has been the highest rated restaurant in the Coachella Valley since its inception.

Le Vallauris is the Coachella Valley's Highest Rated Restaurant by Zagat Guide in Food, Service and Ambiance, and is also the recipient of AAA's 4 Diamond Award.

## About Le Vallauris

Le Vallauris is the leading Coachella Valley restaurant in French - Mediterranean cuisine. Its elegant French-Mediterranean menu, paired with impeccable service, sets a tone of timeless sophistication, with guests raving about the classic dishes, from Dover sole to filet mignon, and the romantic garden patio, shaded by ficus trees and surrounded by fresh flowers.

## Notable Recognition

Jerry Berns, longtime owner of New York's fabled 21 Club, called it "one of the best restaurants in the world." Additionally, Le Vallauris is a gorgeous, Zagat-rated eatery housed in one of Palm Springs' most beautiful and historic homes.

Le Vallauris stands out among Coachella Valley fine dining establishments as a premier French restaurant with enduring recognition and acclaim.

content.text: I have enough to make a confident assessment. Let me finalize.

confirms: Review volume: Yelp ~802 reviews (matches '800+' claim), Tripadvisor ~1,100 reviews, OpenTable 5,706 reviews (well over 5,000) — aggregate ~7,600+, well over 5,000.
Sentiment: mostly positive with mixed trend — Tripadvisor 4.5 stars #7-9 of ~300 restaurants; reviewers praise patio/setting but cite inconsistent service and post-Soho-House quality decline.
Longevity: Founded by Paul Bruggemans in 1973/74 in the 1927 Roberson house — confirmed by multiple sources including Visit Palm Springs and Le Vallauris's own legacy materials.

Ownership shift: Ron Burkle acquired property bundle (Le Vallauris, O'Donnell House at The Willows, Colony 29) and restaurant now appears under Soho House restaurants portfolio (sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris). Confirmed via Palm Springs Post and Soho House site.
OpenTable Top 100 Most Popular Restaurants for Outdoor Dining in America (2023) — confirmed via PS Tribune coverage. The 2022 award is plausible but I only directly verified 2023.
Tripadvisor recognition — confirmed as 'Travelers' Choice' award (which is the current iteration of the Certificate of Excellence program).

Site structured-data gap: levallauris.com 301-redirects to sohohouse.com/restaurants/le-vallauris. That Soho House page has NO JSON-LD / schema.org Restaurant markup, no machine-readable menu (daily chalkboard), only narrative hours/address. Agent-extractable signal lives on third-party platforms.

Query surfacing: Le Vallauris consistently appears in 'best French restaurant Palm Springs', 'fine dining Palm Springs', 'historic restaurants Palm Springs', 'date night', and 'best fine dining Coachella Valley' roundups across Tripadvisor, OpenTable, Visit Palm Springs, Palm Springs Life, Wanderlog, Cavallo Ranch, PS Tribune.
Visibility gap score of 6 and anchor-reference tier — consistent with the pattern of strong third-party citation but a missing canonical site with structured data.
contradicts: Chef Jean-Paul Lair 'long tenure (~29 years per legacy sources)' is presented as current; LinkedIn lists him as 'ex chef - le vallauris', suggesting he has departed. The 29-year tenure is legacy-accurate but the implication of current incumbency is likely outdated. Brief should treat current chef as unknown rather than asserting Lair.
flagged_as_unverified: OpenTable Top 100 Brunch Spots (2022) — could not independently confirm in this pass.
OpenTable 100 Best Al Fresco Dining Restaurants in America (2017) — could not independently confirm.
OpenTable Top 100 Outdoor Dining 2022 specifically (2023 confirmed; 2022 not directly verified).
AAA Four Diamond Award — appears in legacy/marketing copy but no current-year AAA listing was confirmed. Flag as legacy.
Zagat 'Extraordinary to Perfection' rating — legacy; Zagat as an active rating body has effectively ceased printed guides, so the rating is historical at best.

Soho House 'operates' the restaurant — Burkle's ownership of the property is confirmed and the page lives on sohohouse.com, but whether day-to-day operations are Soho House-run vs. simply branded/portfolio-listed is ambiguous in public sources. The planned Palm Springs Soho House club was canceled, which complicates the operational-ownership picture.
input.notes: Core thesis (high review volume across third-party platforms, missing canonical site with no JSON-LD on the Soho House landing page, strong regional query surfacing, anchor-reference tier with visibility gap ~6) holds up well under independent checks. Main correction: drop or qualify the Jean-Paul Lair 'current chef ~29 years' framing — LinkedIn flags him as ex-chef. Several credentials (AAA 4-Diamond current year, Zagat rating, 2017/2022 OpenTable lists) are legacy/marketing-sourced and should be presented as historical rather than current. The Soho House operational angle should be softened to 'Burkle-owned, Soho House-portfolio-listed' rather than 'Soho House-operated' absent clearer evidence.
